ground_truth.title: Best Video Editing Software for Creators
ground_truth.author: Colin Gray
ground_truth.main_content:
If you're creating video, you're also editing it. And that's where it can start to feel complicated! Video editing goes deep, and a glance at the best video editing software tends to be less 'go-kart' and more 'airplane cockpit.' This guide explores what you really need to get those videos polished up and out into the world, covering the best video editing software options available right now.

Standalone video editing software on your computer tends to offer more power and speed, but requires a good computer to run it and can be expensive. Online video editing apps handle files in the cloud, offer more assistance and AI features, but are slower and more clunky for long-form work. For full-length video creators, downloaded standalone software wins with its speed, power and control. For shorts creators or low-edit podcast convertors, online tools offer more assistance and simplicity.

Adobe Premiere is the top choice for long-form video editing on a PC, used by professional filmmakers and amateur producers alike. It offers pinnacle of video editing functionality but has a steep learning curve with very little assistance or automation. Price is $28 per month or $48 per month as part of Creative Cloud.

Filmora is a great low-budget video editor if you value quick polish over editing power. It has a music library and video stock library built right in, colour filters, transcription, and stylish templates. It lacks some of the speed and polish of Premiere but makes up for it in efficiency and assistance. Price is $99 one-off or $69 annually.

Final Cut Pro is the best-in-class video editor for the Mac, offering everything Premiere can do. It's a one-off purchase of $299, buy once and use forever. Like Premiere, it has a steep learning curve but full power and control over everything.

Camtasia is the best choice for educators using screen recordings and webcam capture, commonly used for tutorials, courses and demos. It offers excellent screen recording and easy editing with callouts to highlight parts of the screen. Price is $299 for a perpetual license.

VEED is a fully-featured online video editing package with excellent shorts and clipping features. It includes transcription generation and subtitling, stock footage, music, and title templates. It's great for fast clipping and creating shorts but not ideal for detailed long-form video editing.

FlexClip is a web-based editing platform built for creators who want to polish up their videos fast. It includes AI-powered tools for generating scripts, matching clips to text, and editing images. Great for repurposing blog posts into video content.

Movavi Video Editor is approachable yet surprisingly capable, baking in helpful automation without taking control away. AI helps with background and noise removal, motion tracking, and automatic subtitle generation in multiple languages. Price ranges from free trial to $94.95 lifetime license.
with: If you're creating video, you're also editing it. And that's where it can start to feel complicated!
Video editing software on your computer tends to offer more power and speed, but only if you have a good enough computer to run it.
Adobe Premiere is the top choice for long-form video editing on a PC, used by professional filmmakers and amateur producers alike.
Filmora is a great low-budget video editor if you value quick polish over editing power.
VEED is a fully-featured online video editing package with excellent shorts and clipping features.
